prompt: Which player holds the MLB record for career hits with 4,256?
explanation: Pete Rose's 4,256 career hits remain the all-time MLB record.

prompt: Which player held the career hits record before Pete Rose surpassed it?
explanation: Ty Cobb's 4,189 hits stood as the record for decades before Rose broke it in 1985.

prompt: Which player holds the modern-era single-season batting average record among qualified hitters, batting .426 in 1901... actually which player hit .440 in 1894, the highest single-season average ever recorded?
explanation: Hugh Duffy's .440 average in 1894 is generally recognized as the highest single-season batting average in MLB history.

prompt: Which player holds the highest career batting average in MLB history (minimum qualifying at-bats)?
explanation: Ty Cobb's career batting average of .366 (some sources .367) is the highest in MLB history.

prompt: Which pitcher holds the record for most career wins in MLB history?
explanation: Cy Young's 511 career wins is a record considered essentially unbreakable in the modern game.

prompt: Which award is named after the pitcher who holds MLB's all-time wins record?
explanation: The Cy Young Award, given to the best pitchers each season, is named after Cy Young.

prompt: Which pitcher struck out a record 20 batters in a nine-inning game, a feat matched by only a few others?
options: Roger Clemens (first to do it, twice)
explanation: Roger Clemens struck out 20 batters in a game twice (1986 and 1996), a feat later matched by Kerry Wood and Max Scherzer.

prompt: Which pitcher struck out 20 batters in a single game as a rookie in 1998, at just 20 years old?
explanation: Kerry Wood's 20-strikeout game against Houston is considered one of the greatest pitching performances ever.

prompt: Which Hall of Fame first baseman hit four home runs in a single nine-inning game in 1932, one of the rare players to accomplish this?
explanation: Lou Gehrig hit four home runs in a single game against the Philadelphia Athletics in June 1932.

prompt: Roughly how many pitchers in MLB history have thrown a no-hitter in their very first career start, an extremely rare feat?
explanation: Only a small handful of pitchers in MLB history have thrown a no-hitter in their major league debut start.

prompt: Which player recorded an unassisted triple play in the World Series, the only one in Series history, in 1920?
explanation: Bill Wambsganss's unassisted triple play for Cleveland remains the only one in World Series history.

prompt: Which pitcher recorded the fastest officially measured pitch in MLB history, at 105.8 mph?
explanation: Aroldis Chapman's 105.8 mph pitch in 2010 remains the fastest ever officially recorded in MLB.

prompt: Which player set the modern-era single-season stolen base record with 130 steals in 1982?
explanation: Rickey Henderson's 130 stolen bases in 1982 remains the single-season record.

prompt: Which player holds the MLB career stolen base record?
explanation: Rickey Henderson's 1,406 career stolen bases is far more than any other player in MLB history.

prompt: What nickname did Rickey Henderson carry throughout his career, reflecting his stolen base dominance?
explanation: Henderson's stolen base records earned him the nickname 'The Man of Steal'.

prompt: Which two brothers combined for the most career home runs by siblings in MLB history?
explanation: Hank and Tommie Aaron combined for the most home runs by brothers, though Hank's total vastly outweighs Tommie's.

prompt: Which relief pitcher's career regular-season ERA of 2.21 is among the lowest of any pitcher with significant innings in the modern era?
explanation: Rivera's 2.21 career ERA across 19 seasons is remarkable given his longevity and workload as a closer.

prompt: Which relief pitcher holds the MLB career saves record?
explanation: Mariano Rivera's 652 career saves is the all-time MLB record.

prompt: Which relief pitcher held the career saves record before Mariano Rivera surpassed it?
explanation: Trevor Hoffman's 601 saves stood as the record until Rivera passed him in 2011.

prompt: Which pitcher is renowned for his dominant postseason record with the Yankees, posting a 0.70 career postseason ERA?
explanation: Rivera's postseason dominance, especially in high-pressure save situations, is considered unmatched in baseball history.

prompt: Which pitcher holds the record for most career wins by a left-handed pitcher, with 363?
explanation: Warren Spahn's 363 wins is the most by any left-handed pitcher in MLB history.

prompt: Which player holds the record for the longest home run ever measured, at 575 feet?
explanation: Mickey Mantle's 1953 home run at Griffith Stadium is often cited as the longest measured, at 575 feet.

prompt: Which player set the Statcast-era (since 2015) record for hardest-hit ball, exceeding 122 mph?
explanation: Oneil Cruz set the Statcast-era exit velocity record with a 122.4 mph batted ball in 2022.

prompt: Which player set the Statcast-era record for fastest home-to-first sprint time, showcasing elite speed?
explanation: Elly De La Cruz has recorded some of the fastest sprint speeds ever tracked by Statcast.

prompt: Which player holds the MLB career walks record?
explanation: Barry Bonds's 2,558 career walks, boosted by intentional walks during his peak, is an MLB record.

prompt: Which player was intentionally walked a record number of times in a single season (120 times in 2004)?
explanation: Bonds's 120 intentional walks in 2004 reflect how thoroughly opposing teams avoided pitching to him.

prompt: Which Cincinnati Reds pitcher threw a no-hitter while also hitting a home run in the same 1971 game, a rare double feat?
explanation: Rick Wise threw a no-hitter and hit two home runs in the same game in June 1971, an extraordinary combined feat.

prompt: Which team's late-1990s to early-2000s dynasty won four World Series titles in five years (1996, 1998, 1999, 2000)?
explanation: The Yankees' dynasty under Joe Torre won titles in 1996, 1998, 1999, and 2000.

prompt: Which manager led the New York Yankees dynasty of the late 1990s, winning four titles in five years?
explanation: Joe Torre managed the Yankees to four championships between 1996 and 2000.

prompt: What is the informal term for a batter striking out five times in a single game, an extension of the four-strikeout 'golden sombrero'?
explanation: A fifth strikeout in one game is jokingly referred to as a 'platinum sombrero'.

prompt: What is the informal term for a batter striking out four times in a single game?
explanation: Striking out four times in one game earned the tongue-in-cheek nickname 'golden sombrero'.

prompt: Which term describes leading the league in batting average, home runs, and RBIs in the same season?
explanation: Winning the Triple Crown means leading the league in all three major hitting categories in a single season.

prompt: Which player won the AL Triple Crown in 2012, the first player to do so in 45 years?
explanation: Miguel Cabrera's 2012 Triple Crown was the first in the American League since Carl Yastrzemski in 1967.

prompt: Which player won the AL Triple Crown in 1967, the last to do so before Cabrera's 2012 feat?
explanation: Carl Yastrzemski's 1967 Triple Crown stood as the last in MLB for 45 years.

statement: Cy Young holds the MLB record for most career wins by a pitcher, with 511.
explanation: Young's win total is considered essentially unbreakable given modern pitching usage patterns.

statement: Rickey Henderson holds both the single-season and career stolen base records in MLB.
explanation: Henderson's 130 steals in 1982 and 1,406 career steals are both MLB records.

statement: Mariano Rivera holds the MLB career saves record.
explanation: Rivera's 652 saves is the most in MLB history.

statement: Miguel Cabrera won the American League Triple Crown in 2012, the first player to do so in 45 years.
explanation: Cabrera led the AL in batting average, home runs, and RBIs that season.

statement: Bill Wambsganss recorded the only unassisted triple play in World Series history.
explanation: This rare feat occurred during the 1920 World Series.

statement: Aroldis Chapman holds the record for the fastest officially recorded pitch in MLB history.
explanation: Chapman's 105.8 mph fastball in 2010 remains the record.

statement: Ty Cobb held the career hits record before Pete Rose surpassed it.
explanation: Cobb's 4,189 hits stood as the record until Rose broke it in 1985.

statement: Kerry Wood struck out 20 batters in a game as a rookie in 1998.
explanation: Wood's 20-strikeout performance against Houston is regarded as one of the greatest pitching feats ever.

statement: Barry Bonds holds the MLB career walks record.
explanation: Bonds's 2,558 career walks reflect both his plate discipline and opposing teams' reluctance to pitch to him.

statement: Hugh Duffy's .440 batting average in 1894 is generally considered the highest single-season average in MLB history.
explanation: Duffy's mark from the high-offense 1894 season remains a historical record.

explanation: Henderson's speed and base-stealing prowess remain unmatched in MLB history.

explanation: Cabrera's rare Triple Crown season made him one of the most decorated hitters of his generation.

Holds the all-time MLB wins record with 511
The pitcher-of-the-year award in both leagues is named after him

explanation: Young's win total remains one of the most unbreakable records in professional sports.

prompt: Order these MLB career record achievements chronologically by when they were set (earliest first).
events: Ty Cobb sets the career hits record (retires 1928)
Cy Young's career wins record is completed (retires 1911, note: record set before Cobb's hits record year, included for order testing)
Pete Rose breaks Cobb's career hits record (1985)
Rickey Henderson breaks Lou Brock's career stolen base record (1991)
explanation: Chronologically: Young's career ends 1911, Cobb's hits record set by 1928, Rose breaks it in 1985, Henderson breaks the steals record in 1991.

prompt: Which pitcher recorded the modern-era record for most consecutive scoreless innings pitched, with 59 in 1968?
explanation: Don Drysdale's 58 (often cited as 58 or 59 depending on source) consecutive scoreless innings record stood until Hershiser broke it in 1988.

prompt: Which pitcher broke Don Drysdale's scoreless innings streak record in 1988, with 59 consecutive scoreless innings?
explanation: Orel Hershiser's 59 consecutive scoreless innings in 1988 broke Drysdale's two-decade-old record.

prompt: Which pitcher posted the lowest single-season ERA of the live-ball era (post-1920), with a 1.12 ERA in 1968?
explanation: Bob Gibson's remarkable 1.12 ERA in 1968 is the lowest of the live-ball era, contributing to MLB lowering the pitcher's mound afterward.

prompt: Which rule change did MLB make after the historically low-scoring 1968 season, partly a response to Bob Gibson's dominance?
options: Banning the spitball entirely (already banned earlier)
explanation: MLB lowered the mound from 15 to 10 inches after 1968's 'Year of the Pitcher' to help increase offense.

prompt: What is the common nickname for the 1968 MLB season, given its historically low offensive numbers?
explanation: 1968 is remembered as 'The Year of the Pitcher' due to dominant pitching performances league-wide.

prompt: Which player won the NL MVP in the 'Year of the Pitcher' (1968) as a pitcher himself?
explanation: Bob Gibson won the NL MVP and Cy Young in 1968, largely due to his historic 1.12 ERA season.

prompt: Which pitcher won 31 games in the 1968 season, the last pitcher to win 30+ games in a single MLB season?
explanation: Denny McLain's 31-win season for Detroit in 1968 remains the last 30-win season in MLB history.

prompt: Which pitcher last won 30 or more games in a single MLB season before Denny McLain, having done so decades earlier?
explanation: Dizzy Dean's 30-win season in 1934 was the only other one in the modern era before McLain's 1968 feat.

prompt: Which pitcher holds the modern-era single-season strikeout record with 383 in 1973?
explanation: Nolan Ryan struck out 383 batters in the 1973 season, a modern-era record.

prompt: Which pitcher holds the single-season strikeout record of the 19th century, with 513 strikeouts in 1886 under different-era rules?
explanation: Matt Kilroy's 513 strikeouts in 1886 came under different pitching distance and rules than the modern game.

prompt: Which pitcher won a record 59 games in a single season in 1884, under 19th-century rules very different from today?
explanation: Old Hoss Radbourn's 59 wins in 1884 came during an era of very different pitching rules and workloads.

prompt: Which award, given to the top defensive player at each position, was first introduced by Rawlings in 1957?
explanation: The Gold Glove Award has been given annually by Rawlings since 1957.

prompt: Which award, first given in 2011, recognizes the single best defensive player across all of MLB regardless of position?
options: Wilson Defensive Player of the Year Award
explanation: The Platinum Glove Award, introduced in 2011, honors the best overall defensive player in each league.

prompt: Which pitcher holds the all-time record for most career Gold Glove Awards, with 18, more than any position player?
explanation: Greg Maddux's 18 Gold Gloves is the all-time MLB record across any position.

prompt: Which shortstop is famous for his defensive wizardry and backflips, winning 13 Gold Glove Awards?
explanation: Ozzie Smith, nicknamed 'The Wizard', won 13 Gold Gloves and was famous for his acrobatic defense.

prompt: Which catcher won 13 Gold Glove Awards, tied for among the most at his position in MLB history?
explanation: Ivan Rodriguez won 13 Gold Gloves, reflecting his defensive excellence as a catcher.

prompt: Which player is credited with the most career defensive Wins Above Replacement (dWAR) at shortstop?
explanation: Ozzie Smith's defensive value at shortstop is regarded among the greatest ever measured.

prompt: Has any pitcher in MLB history ever thrown a no-hitter in each of his first two career starts?
options: No, this has never happened in MLB history
Yes, it happened once in the 1890s
Yes, it has happened three times
Yes, it happens roughly once per decade
explanation: No pitcher has ever thrown no-hitters in each of their first two career starts; this remains an unachieved feat in MLB history.

prompt: Which pitcher threw a no-hitter in his very first career start, becoming a rare member of an elite group, pitching for Cincinnati in 1892?
explanation: Bumpus Jones threw a no-hitter in his major league debut in October 1892, one of the earliest examples of this rare feat.

prompt: Which pitcher threw a no-hitter in his first career start in the modern era, pitching for the St. Louis Browns in 1953?
explanation: Bobo Holloman's 1953 debut no-hitter remains one of the most improbable feats in modern MLB history.

prompt: Which Hall of Fame pitcher, a Cy Young winner who played primarily for the Cubs and Rangers, never won a World Series title during his career?
explanation: Ferguson Jenkins, the 1971 NL Cy Young winner, never won a World Series title during his career.

prompt: Despite a legendary 300-win, seven-no-hitter career, which pitcher never won a World Series title?
explanation: Nolan Ryan never won a World Series title, despite his Hall of Fame career and record seven no-hitters.

prompt: Which team's 1927 roster, featuring Babe Ruth and Lou Gehrig, is often cited as the greatest team in MLB history, nicknamed 'Murderers' Row'?
explanation: The 1927 Yankees, featuring Ruth, Gehrig and a dominant lineup, are frequently ranked as the best team ever assembled.

prompt: What nickname was given to the powerful 1927 New York Yankees lineup?
explanation: 'Murderers' Row' specifically refers to the devastating 1927 Yankees batting lineup.

prompt: Which team won a record 116 games in the 1906 regular season, still an MLB record for wins in a 154-game season?
explanation: The 1906 Cubs won 116 games, still the record for a 154-game season.

prompt: Which team won a modern-era record 116 games during the 2001 season in a 162-game schedule?
explanation: The 2001 Mariners won 116 games, a mark later matched by the 2022 Los Angeles Dodgers.

prompt: Which team won a record 114 games during the 1998 season en route to a World Series title, one of the winningest teams ever?
explanation: The 1998 Yankees won 114 games in the regular season and then swept the World Series.

prompt: Which pitcher's win total of 511 is considered so unbreakable that no active pitcher has come remotely close in the modern bullpen era?
explanation: Cy Young's 511 wins came in an era of far heavier pitcher workloads than exist today.

prompt: Which pitcher is second on the all-time MLB career wins list, with 417?
explanation: Walter Johnson's 417 career wins ranks second only to Cy Young in MLB history.

prompt: Which pitcher, nicknamed 'The Big Train', spent his entire 21-year career with the Washington Senators?
explanation: Walter Johnson was known as 'The Big Train' and remained loyal to Washington for his entire career.

prompt: Which pitcher holds the all-time MLB career strikeout record with 5,714?
explanation: Nolan Ryan's 5,714 career strikeouts is far more than any other pitcher in MLB history.

prompt: Which pitcher is second on the all-time career strikeout list, with 4,875?
explanation: Randy Johnson's 4,875 strikeouts rank second behind only Nolan Ryan.

prompt: Which slugger's single-season home run record of 73 (set in 2001) remains the most in MLB history despite steroid-era controversy?
explanation: Barry Bonds hit 73 home runs in 2001, a single-season record that still stands despite ongoing controversy over the era.

prompt: Which two sluggers' dramatic 1998 home run chase, ending with 70 and 66 home runs respectively, is credited with reviving MLB's popularity after a labor dispute?
options: Barry Bonds and Ken Griffey Jr.
explanation: McGwire (70) and Sosa (66) captivated the nation during the 1998 home run race, helping restore fan interest after the 1994 strike.

statement: Barry Bonds's single-season home run record of 73, set in 2001, still stands as the MLB record today.
explanation: Despite steroid-era scrutiny, Bonds's 73-home-run season in 2001 remains the official single-season record.
